by_judge.judge: CHIEF JUSTICE & K.S. HEMALEKHA & VIJAYKUMAR A. PATIL

narrative:
On July 2, 2026, the Karnataka High Court demonstrated an increased hearing rate of 49.3%, with 52 judges hearing 1,197 cases out of 2,430 scheduled. This performance represents a clear rise in conversion efficiency from the previous days; the hearing rate stood at 42.8% on July 1 (1,031 of 2,407 scheduled) and 41.2% on June 30 (974 of 2,362 scheduled).

Among individual judges, Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the heaviest docket, successfully hearing 103 of 239 scheduled cases (43.1%). Justice V. Srishananda in Hall 21 achieved a strong conversion rate, hearing 70 of 94 scheduled cases (74.5%) in his primary listing. Additionally, Justice Shivashankar Amarannavar in Hall 20 displayed robust clearance by hearing 61 of 91 scheduled cases (67.0%). 

From a procedural stage perspective, "Orders" remained the dominant stage with 829 scheduled cases, of which 466 were heard (56.2%). This shows improved efficiency compared to July 1, when the conversion rate for "Orders" was 45.5% (324 of 712). Meanwhile, "Admission" stages converted at 53.2% (210 of 395), whereas "Final Hearing" stages lagged behind with only 25.1% (123 of 490) of scheduled cases heard.

In terms of case types, Writ Petitions (WP) continued to make up the largest share of the docket. Out of 1,021 scheduled WPs, 486 were heard (47.6%), marking a notable increase in clearance efficiency from the 36.6% conversion rate recorded on July 1. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) saw 155 of 306 cases heard (50.7%), while Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) ended the session with a 39.9% hearing rate (117 of 293).
